content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 21, 1992

Janet Tracy
City Clerk
City of Sausalito
P.O. Box 1279
Sausalito, CA  94966

Re: Your Request For Confirmation
Of Telephone Advice
Our File No. A-92-269

Dear Ms. Tracy:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided
to you concerning the filing obligations of Sausalito City
Councilmember Amy Belser under the conflict of interest disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

Ms. Belser was a planning commissioner until April 21, 1992,
when she assumed her city council office. You asked if Ms. Belser
is required to file a leaving office statement as a former
planning commissioner and an assuming office statement as a newly
elected city councilmember.

Section 87205 states that a person who completes a term of an
office specified in Section 87200 and on the same day begins a
term of the same office or another such office of the same
jurisdiction is not deemed to assume office or leave office.

Therefore, since there was no break in service between the
time she held the office of planning commissioner and the time she
was elected to city council and both positions are listed in
Section 87200, Ms. Belser is not required to complete a leaving
office statement or an assuming office statement.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento CA 95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Janet Tracy
A-92-269
Page 2


If I can be of further assistance, please contact me at
(916) 322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature]
                                        By: Colleen McGee
                                           Political Reform Consultant
                                           Technical Assistance and
                                           Analysis Division

sections.question: You asked if Ms. Belser is required to file a leaving office statement as a former planning commissioner and an assuming office statement as a newly elected city councilmember.
sections.conclusion: Since there was no break in service between the time she held the office of planning commissioner and the time she was elected to city council and both positions are listed in Section 87200, Ms. Belser is not required to complete a leaving office statement or an assuming office statement.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a person who transitions from planning commissioner to city councilmember on the same day must file leaving office and assuming office statements under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under Government Code Section 87205, no filing statements are required because there was no break in service between the two offices on the same day, and both positions are specified offices under the disclosure provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Clear advice letter with explicit question and conclusion sections. Document confirms prior telephone advice. File number A-92-269 matches document ID 92-269.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked if Ms. Belser is required to file a leaving office statement as a former planning commissioner and an assuming office statement as a newly elected city councilmember.

CONCLUSION: Since there was no break in service between the time she held the office of planning commissioner and the time she was elected to city council and both positions are listed in Section 87200, Ms. Belser is not required to complete a leaving office statement or an assuming office statement.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirms telephone advice that City Councilmember Amy Belser is not required to file leaving or assuming office statements when transitioning from planning commissioner to city council on the same day, as Section 87205 deems there to be no break in service.
